title: Alsine procera
taxonomy: Alsine procera is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Caryophyllaceae. It is a member of the genus Alsine, which is a group of small, annual or perennial plants. The Caryophyllaceae family is a large and diverse group of plants, commonly known as the pink or carnation family. Alsine procera is native to Europe and Western Asia, where it grows in a variety of habitats. The plant's classification reflects its evolutionary relationships and shared characteristics with other members of the Caryophyllaceae family.

intro: Alsine procera, also known as Alsine or Purple Crowberry, is a low-growing, perennial plant species native to mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of digestive issues, wounds, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Alsine procera include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and glycosides, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Alsine procera is often prepared as a tincture, infusion, or decoction, and is commonly used to treat various ailments, including digestive issues, fever, and skin conditions.
